preview_text:
The model favours Norway at 43%, with Senegal the underdog at 26%. A draw sits between them at 31%, making this a relatively open contest rather than a lopsided matchup. Norway hold a meaningful but not dominant edge in the forecast.

The spread across outcomes reveals a competitive fixture. Norway's advantage is real but modest—no single result commands majority probability. A draw is plausible and sits closer to the favourites' probability than would be the case in a one-sided match, suggesting the teams are reasonably well-matched on the model's view.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model. Norway win stands at 44% in the market versus 43% in the model, and the draw probabilities differ by under 4 percentage points. Senegal's chances diverge slightly more, with the market at 28% against the model's 26%, but the gap remains narrow. Polymarket shows notably different pricing—a three-way near-parity at roughly 33% each—suggesting crypto traders assign less confidence to Norway's edge. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources.
